description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The body is constructed from dark brown wooden staves, each tightly bound by three prominent metallic hoops—one near the top, one at the center, and one near the bottom. The wood grain is visible, giving the surface a natural, slightly weathered appearance, and there are subtle scuff marks and stains that suggest prior use. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top appearing to be securely closed and flush with the rim. The metallic hoops are a dull gray, showing minor signs of wear but no significant rust or damage. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A sturdy, dark brown wooden barrel with three metallic hoops and a closed, flat top. The wood shows natural grain and wear, while the metal bands reinforce the structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with all three metal hoops and the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the seamless join of the top and the upper metal hoop, with minor scuff marks on the wood.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the central bulge and the even spacing of the metal hoops around the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a patch of darker discoloration on the wood and the continuous metal reinforcement.
